name: ​Zoho Books
description:
__POSSIBLE_UNTRANSLATED__

What Zoho Books Does

If you want your billing to live inside your actual accounting software (the tool your CPA uses), Zoho Books is the answer. It’s the "QuickBooks Alternative" that is actually built for the modern web. ​It’s a full-scale accounting package. It handles everything from bank reconciliation and VAT/Sales Tax to inventory and recurring billing.

Source: Zoho Books

​Key Features

🏆 Client Portal: A secure place for clients to see all their past invoices and current projects.

🏆 Bank Sync: Automatically pulls in your bank transactions for easy reconciliation.

🏆 Workflow Rules: Highly technical "If/Then" logic for almost any action in the system.

​Benefits of Zoho Books

​It scales with you. As your agency grows to 50+ employees and complex tax requirements, Zoho Books won't break. It also integrates perfectly with the rest of the massive Zoho ecosystem.

​Pricing

  • ​Standard: $20/mo (billed annually)
  • ​Professional: $50/mo (billed annually)
  • ​Premium: $70/mo (billed annually)

There is a free plan available as well. Discounts are available when choosing to bill annually.

​Pros

​✅ The most "complete" financial tool on this list.

✅ Excellent automation for bank reconciliation.

​Cons

​🚫 Can be overwhelming for non-accountants.

🚫 Customer support is famously slow unless you are on a high-tier plan.

‍

What Customers Say

​"What stands out for me is the invoicing automation, including recurring invoices and the ability to connect bank reconciliation with the bank server."

– Review collected by and hosted on G2.com.
summing_up_description:
__POSSIBLE_UNTRANSLATED__

​Summing Up…

Choosing a recurring billing system is about more than automating invoices. The right platform influences how smoothly an agency scales, how easily teams manage ongoing work, and how predictable monthly revenue becomes.

Agencies that prioritize operational visibility and long-term profitability often benefit from a system like Synup OS, where billing is closely connected to client management and service delivery. Teams that place greater emphasis on design-forward proposals and client-facing experiences may prefer HoneyBook. For agencies that require white-label flexibility and support for multi-location clients, Synup remains a strong option worth considering.

‍

​Why Synup is the Strategic Choice

​For the mid-sized agency owner serving local businesses, Synup remains the most logical choice. That's because it’s the only tool that understands that billing is just one part of the service.

​By combining your CRM, your local SEO tools, your reputation management, and your recurring billing into one "Agency Operating System," you eliminate the "app tax" (paying for 10 different subscriptions) and the "attention tax" (switching between 10 different tabs).

Synup OS lets you automate everything from the initial proposal to the final payment. With built-in e-signatures, automated dunning, and a white-labeled client portal, you can finally focus on what you do best: growing your clients' businesses. ​Get started with Synup now. 

‍

FAQs

  1. ​What's a good billing system for both one-time and recurring payments?

​Tools like Synup and Stripe Billing are the most flexible here. They allow you to bundle a one-time "Setup Fee" with a "Monthly Management" retainer in the same invoice, giving your client a single, easy checkout experience.

  1. ​Which software is best for billing?

​There is no "best," only "best for you." If you need accounting-grade depth, Zoho Books is top-tier. If you need a tool that actually helps you fulfill agency work while billing, Synup is the industry leader for marketing agencies.

  1. ​Is CRM a billing system?

​Technically, no, but the lines are blurring. Modern "Agency Operating Systems" like Synup combine CRM (managing relationships) with Billing (managing money) because, in an agency, those two things are inseparable. Using a CRM that has a billing system built in is usually much more efficient than trying to sync two separate platforms.

‍
